titleStmt.title: SII 32, part 2, no. 204: original edition by S. Swaminathan
No. 204. MADRAS MUSEUM PLATES.

summary.p: Plates now deposited with the Madras Museum.

It is the only copper plate of Uttama-chōḷa. A few plates of the set are missing at the beginning and one at end and they would have supplied many more details about the reigning king and his times. The portion of the record on the existing five copper-plates consists of a short Sanskrit prose passage (lines 1-6) three Sanskrit verses (lines 6-11) and Tamil prose (lines 11-121).

The Sanskrit portion records that 200 pieces of gold were deposited with the two classes of paṭṭaśālins residing in the quarters of Kachchippēḍu, known as Kampuḻānpāḍi, Kaṁsahappāḍi, Atimānappāḍi and Eṟṟuvaḻichchēri. The residents of the above two quarters were appointed supervisors of the temple by the king Uttama-chōḷa. The latter's predecessor Parāntaka chōḷa I on an earlier occasion ordered that the residents of Śōḻāniyamam, another ward at Kachchippēḍu, should remit two prastha and a kuḍubaka of rice and one prastha of oil to the god at Uraka and they were exempted from all other taxes payable to the king. These residents of Śōḻaniyamam together with the officials of the Ūraka temple should maintain in turn the temple accounts and show them every month to the weavers of royal garments residing in the above four quarters.

The Tamil portion records that in his 16th regnal year (987 A.D.) when the king Uttama-chōḷa was seated in the chitra-maṇḍapa in his palace, at Kachchippēḍu his officer Sikkal-uḍaiyāṉ Nakkaṉ Kaṇichchaṉ alias Śōḻa-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ petitioned the king that the income of the temple at Ūrakam consisted of kōlniraikūli and kālaḷavukūli collected at Kachchippēḍu 2) the yield of the lands purchased by the temple at Kachchippēḍu and Tuṇḍuṇakkachchēri 3) the interest (in paddy and money) accruing on investments made by the temple with the three assemblies might be apportioned for the several services in the main temple and in the shrine called Karikālateṟṟi. The residents of two quarters of Kachchippēḍu ie Kambuḻāṉpāḍi and Atimānappāḍi appointed to supervise and carry out this apportionment. The king entrusted the matter with the chief who made the request and the latter settled the required apportionment of income.
The lands purchased and investments made on earlier occasions are detailed with references to the earlier inscriptions are given below.

table.c3: The assemblies of Kūram and Ariyarperumbākkam received 250 kaḻañju of gold from the temple and had agreed to measure every year as interest thereon five hundred kāḍi of paddy.
The assembly of Uḻaiyūr received fifty kaḻañju of gold and they agreed to measure annually one hunded and fifty kāḍi of paddy.
The assembly of Oḻukkaippākkam received 24 kaḻañju of gold and agreed to pay an interest 1 kaḻañju and 4 mañjāḍi of gold to the temple.

summary.p: Thus the income derived from the taxes kālaḷavu and kōlniṟaikūli, the yield from the land and the annual interest remitted by the assemblies-were to be utilized to carry out the temple services, under the supervision of the official Mūvēndavēḷāṉ.

The plates further state that in the sixteenth regnal year of Parakēsarivarmaṉ (Uttama-chōḷa) a new endowment had been created for conducting the Chittirai tirunāḷ festival at the temple of Ūrakam. For this purpose 200 kaḻañju of gold were deposited with the residents of Kambuḻāṉpāḍi - 73(1/2) kaḻañju Atimāṉappāḍi - 73(1/2) kaḻañju Kañjagappāḍi - 35 kaḻañju Eṟṟuvaḻichēri - 18 kaḻañju
And the annual interest thirty-kaḻañju of gold accrued from these investments were to be defrayed for meeting the expenditure in conducting the Chittirai festival at the temple.

The plates then proceed to mention that in the eighteenth regnal year of Parakēsarivarman, who took Madurai and Īḻam (Parāntaka I), a concession had been granted as per the old tradition, to the residents Tōḷāchcheviyar alias Elākkaiyār who settled at Śōḻaniyamam on account of their small numbers. And those who came from outside and settled at the same locality were to remit an uḻakku of oil and two nāḻi of rice every month as taxes to the temple. It was also stipulated that they also need not pay any other taxes. It was also enjoined that during the sixteenth regnal year of Uttama chōḷa the residents of the quarter Śōḻiyaniyamam should also provide an accountant to the temple and he was to receive daily one kuṟuṇi of paddy from the temple treasury and two kaḻañju of gold annually.

The charter then mentions that three Saṅkarappāḍiyārs, Raṇajayapāḍi, Ekavīrappāḍi and Vāmana-Saṅkarappāḍi of Kachchippēḍu received 20 kaḻañju of gold for maintaining a perpetual lamp. It is mentioned that it is to be burnt by the residents of Raṇajayappāḍi, also to burn a twilight lamp with the oil supplied by the Śōḻaniyamattār.

The charter also made provisions to conduct festivals of Uttarāyaṇa-saṅkrānti and Chitra-vishu out of the funds earmarked under miscellaneous head. If there were any hinderances in the conduct of festivals the eighteen nāṭṭārs should solve the same. Also the plates stipulate that the administrator of nagaram (nagaram-āḷvāṉ), the members of the Annual supervision Committee and the residents of Eṟṟuvaḻichchēri and Kañjagappāḍi were required to check the accounts at the end of every festival. The residents of the above two quarters were to appoint a watchman out of the endowments deposited in the treasury. The nagaram was further vested with in authority of appointing officials to manage the temple affairs, the watchman and the accountants and exempting them from payment of all taxes. It was also laid down that if they did not obtain any suitable person well-versed in temple worship, a brāhmaṇa versed in the vēdas should be appointed to perform the worship. The madhyastha of Vīrappāḍi, Nārpatteṇṇāyira Maṅgalādittan figures as signatory.
Published in S.I.I., Vol. III No. 128.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
yatisma . sa ca teṣām yugmavamśa paṭṭaśālinān=teṣveva karuvuḷān pāṭikamsahappā
dyatimānappādyeṟṟu vaḻiccerītyākhyāteṣu vāṭakeṣu tathā taddhemaśatadvayannyadha
tta teṣvava vāṭakeṣvatimānappāṭikaruvuḷān pāṭuti vāṭakadva yajātām stan=tuvāyā
n=tasyaiva harestulāprasthādimānasam bhuteṣvartdheṣu hemapṛddhyu panateṣvarttheṣu
cāyavyayālokinām śrīkāryyam kratāmabhāvāt sa Evarājā śrīkāryyakara
ṇāya tāneva svayannya yuṅtaḥ . dātavya mmāsamātra kuḍuba kasahitan=taṇḍu
laprasthayugman=taila prasthañca coḻāniya nilayanairurakasthatri yāmne . nā
deyam rājabhāvyam karamiti madhuronmāthinā śūnyabhāvāccoḷenājñā pitai
stairatha nagarajanairavya nujñātametat . Āyavyathālikhya coḻāniyamavā
sibhiḥ . māsakrameṇa caikaikandarśanīyau kaḍumbibhiḥ rājavastrakṛtāmeṣāñcatu
rvvāṭanivāsinām . hareḥ kāryyaniyuktaiśca sārndhamūrakavāsinaḥ . śrī koppara
kecaripanmarāṉa śrī Uttamacoḻadevarkku yāṇṭu patiṉāṟāvatu Uṭai
Plate I b.

yār kaccippeṭṭu koyiliṉuḷḷāl teṟkil cittira maṇṭapatteḻun
taruḷi Irukka Atikārikaḷ coḻamūventaveḷār Emperumāṉ Ikkaccippeṭṭu
Ūrakattu niṉṟaruḷiṉa devarkku Ikkaccippeṭṭuk kolniṟai kūliyum kāla
ḷavukūliyum Ivarkku pokamāy varum maṟṟum Ittevarkke kaccippeṭṭum tu
ṇṭuṇukkacceriyilum vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya bhūmiyum maṟṟum poli
ūṭṭuḷḷaṉavum muṉpu Ittevarkku nivantañ ceytilāmaiyi
l nivantañ ceyyum Ikkaccippeṭṭu Iraṇṭu ceri Ittevaruṭai
ya śrīkāriyaṅkaṭaikkāṇavum Aruḷicceyvateṉṟu viṇṇappañ ceyya . I
kkaccippeṭṭu Ūrakattu niṉṟaruḷiṉa devarkku Ivvūr kolniṟai kūliyum kālaḷa
vu kūliyum vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya bhumikaḷum poli Ūṭṭuḷḷaṉavum nīyey ni
van=tañ ceyvīyeṉṟum Ivvūr kampuḻāṉpāṭiyum Atimāṉapāṭiyum I
vviraṇṭu ceriyum Idevar śrī kāriyamārāyavum Ipparicu nivantañ ceyka ve
Plate II a.

ṉṟum Aruḷicceyya Atikāri cikkaruṭaiyāṉ nakkaṉ kaṇiccaṉāṉa co
ḻamuventaveḷāṉ viṇṇappattāl nivan=tañ ceyvittapaṭi kālaḷa
vukūliyum kolniṟaikūliyum Idevar vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya nilaṅkaḷil
pokamum Idevar poliūṭṭu śilālekhaippaṭi koppara kecaripanmaṟku
yāṇṭu Irupattiraṇṭāvatu kūrattu sabhaiyārum Ariyaṟ perumpākkattu
sabhaiyārum koṇṭa poṉ Iruṉūṟṟempatiṉ kaḻañciṉukku
taṅkaḷur Eṇṇāḻip poṟkālāl Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku Āṭṭak ka
ṭava policai nellu Aiññūṟṟuk kāṭiyum Uḻaiyūr sabhaiyār śilā
lekhaippaṭi koṇṭa poṉ Aimpatiṉ kaḻañciṉāl Orāṭṭai nāḷai
kku Aḷakka kaṭava policai nellu ṉūṟṟaimpatiṉ kāṭiyum kovi
caiyakampapanmaṟku yāṇṭu Oṉpatāvatu Oḻukkaipākkattu sabhai
yār śilālekhaippaṭi koṇṭu kaṭava poṉ Irupattu nāṟ kaḻañciṉāl Orā
Plate II b.

ṭṭai nāḷaik kiṭakkaṭava policaip poṉ kaḻañce nālu mañcāṭiyum ni
vantañ ceytapaṭi tiruvamirtu muṉṟu san=tikku nel mukkuṟuṇi Aṟunāḻiyu
m kaṟiyamutu Iraṇṭukku muṉṟu san=tikku nel nāṉāḻiyum neyyamutu nicatam
Uḻakkiṉukku nel Aiññāḻiyum tayiramutu potu Uriyāka mūṉṟu san=tikku ta
yiramutu nāḻi Urikku nel muṉṉāḻiyum Aṭaikkāyamutu mūṉṟu san=tikku
nel muṉṉāḻiyum viṟakiṉukku nel Irunāḻiyum Ārātikkum
vetabrāhmaṇaṉ Oruvaṉukku nel patakkum Ivaṉukku puṭavai mutal
Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku poṉ Aiñkaḻañcum paricārakañ ceyyu māṇi Oruvaṉukku
nel Aṟunāḻiyum Ivaṉukku puṭavaimutal Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku poṉ
kaḻañcum tirumeykāppāṉ Oruvaṉukku nicata nel kuṟuṇiyum Ivaṉu
kku puṭavai mutal Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku poṉṉiru kaḻañcum nan=taṉavaṉam Uḻap
pār Iruvarkku nicata nel kuṟuṇi nāṉāḻiyum Ivarkaḷukku puṭavaikku poṉ
Plate III a.

kaḻañcum caṅkirān=ti Oṉṟiṉukku Ācāryyapūcaṉai Uṭpaṭa poṉ kaḻañcey kālā
ka caṅkirān=ti paṉṉiraṇṭiṉukku poṉ patiṉaiṅkaḻañcum tirumeyp pūccu
kkum tirupukaikkum tiṅkaḷ Araikkāl poṉṉāka Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku
poṉ kaḻañcaraiyum tirunamaṉikai muṉṟukku Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku po
ṉ mukkālum tiruparicaṭṭam mūṉṟukku Orāṭṭaināḷaikku poṉ kaḻañ
cum Ukaccakaḷ talaippaṟai Oṉṟum mattaḷi Iraṇṭum kaṟaṭikai Oṉ
ṟum tāḷam Oṉṟum cekaṇṭikai Oṉṟum kāḷam Iraṇṭum kai
maṇi Oṉṟumāka Āḷ Oṉpatiṉukku puṭavai mutaluṭpaṭa Uḻaiūr poli
Ūṭṭu nel ṉūṟṟaimpatiṉ kāṭiyum kaccippeṭṭu nakarattār pakkal vilaiko
ṇṭuṭaiya nilattil cittiravallipperuñceṟuvāṉa paṭṭiyum tuṇṭu
ṇukkacceriyil vilaikoṇṭuṭaiya nilattil meṭṭumatakāṟu pāñca
cen=taṟaip pottaṉ nilattukku vaṭakkil taṭi muṉṟum kāṭāṭi kuṇ
Plate III b.

ṭiliṉ vaṭakkil ceṟuvu voṉṟum paḷḷamatakāṟu pāñca nilattuḷ
koṉeriyār pokattil vaṭakkil kuṇṭilumāka taṭi Aiñciṉāl pa
ṭṭi nilamumāka Innilam Iraṇṭu paṭṭiyum Ippoliyūṭṭu nel ṉūṟ
ṟaimpatiṉkāṭiyum Uvaccarkaḷ Oṉpatiṉmarkku nivan=tamākavum tirume
ḻukkiṭuvārkku nicata nel muṉṉāḻiyum karikālateṟṟiyil Iruvar devark
ku muṉṟu san=tikku nicatamariciyaṟu nāḻiyāka nicatamarici kuṟuṇi nā
ṉāḻikku nel mukkuṟuṇi Aṟunāḻiyum kaṟiyamutu muṉṟu san=ti
kku nel nāṉāḻiyum viṟakukku nel muṉṉāḻiyum muṉṟu san=tikku neyya
mutu Uḻakkiṉukku nel Aiññāḻiyum Iruvar devarkkum tirunon=tā viḷakki
raṇṭiṉukku neyyurikku ney kuṟuṇi nāṉāḻiyum tirumeyppūccukkum
tirupukaikkum tiṅkaḷ mañcāṭippoṉṉāka Orāṭṭai nāḷaikku poṉ paṉ
ṉiraṇṭu mañcāṭiyum Ivviruvar devarkkum nivandhamākavum koppara kecaripa
Plate IV a.

nmaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉāṟāvatu kaccippeṭṭu Ūrakattu niṉṟaruḷiṉa devar pakkal I
vvūr kampuḻāṉpāṭiyār koṇṭa poṉ Eḻupattumukkaḻañcaraiyum A
timāṉappāṭiyār koṇṭa poṉ Eḻupattumukkaḻañcaraiyum kañcaka
ppāṭiyār koṇṭa poṉ muppattaiṅkaḻañcum Eṟṟuvaḻicceriyār
koṇṭa poṉ patiṉeṇkaḻañcum Ākappoṉ Irunūṟṟukkaḻañ
ciṉukku kaḻañciṉvāy piḷavu policaiyāka Orāṭṭai nā
ḷaikku van=ta policaippoṉ muppatiṉ kaḻañcu Ippo
ṉ muppatiṉ kaḻañcum Idevar cittiraitiruviḻāvukku nivan=tañ ceyta
paṭi tiruviḻā Eḻunāḷaikkum Eṇṇaikku pon Eḻukaḻañcum Eḻu nāḷaikku nā
ṟu pūvum nāṟu cāntukkum poṉ Iru kaḻañcum Eḻunāḷum koṭṭi cey
yum devaraṭiyarkku koṟṟukkum pūcaṉaikkumāka poṉ Aiṅkaḻañcum Eḻu
nāḷum brāhmaṇabhojaṉattukku Aṉṟāṭa +Ignore this symbol.kattāl neṟkoṇṭu
Plate IV b.

Ūṭṭuvatāṉa poṉ patiṉ(k)kaḻañcum devar paḷḷiccivikai kāvuñcivi
kaiyārkkum ciṟappuvanta Uvaccarkkum Eḻunāḷaikku poṉ kaḻañcum
kaṇṭaḻivu poṉ Aiṅkaḻañcum Ākap poṉ ner tiruviḻāvukku viḷakku pi
ṭippārum koṭieṭuppārum kampuḻāṉpāṭiyārum Atimāṉap pāṭiyārum
eṟṟuvaḻicceriyārum kañcakappāṭiyārum Idevarceriyāṉa coḻā niya
mattu muṉpuḷḷa toḷācceviyarāṉa Elākkaiyar Eccaṟṟamai
yil Ivvolakkaiyyār pūrvvamarjjāti Iṟai Iṟukkak kaṭavarallāmai
yil Iccerikku puṟattuniṉṟu vanteṟiṉa kuṭikaḷai maṉaiyāl tiṅkaḷ nāḻi
Uḻakkeṇṇaiyum Irunāḻi Ariciyum Idevarkke yiṟaiyāka koṇṭu maṟṟu Inna
karañ cuṭṭiṉa Iṟai Eppeṟpaṭṭatum koḷḷāti tākavum Ivarkaḷai Itaṉṟi maṟ
ṟu Iṟai kāṭṭiṉār keṅkai Iṭai kumari Iṭai ceytār ceyta pāvaṅ koḷva
tākavum Eṉṟu Ipparicu matiraiyum Īḻamuṅ koṇṭa śrīparakecaripanmaṟku
Plate V a.

yāṇṭu patiṉeṭṭāvatu Ikkaccippeṭṭu nakarattār ceyta vyavasthai paṭiyey I
devarum IvakkaḷaiRead ivarkaḷai Ivviṟaiyey koḷvatākavum Icceriyār Idevarkku
kaṇakku Iṭuvatākavum Ivaṉukku Idevar paṇṭāratte nicatam kuṟuṇi nellum
āṇṭuvarai Irukaḻañcu poṉ Iṭuvatākavum Ivvūr Iraṇajayappāṭi Ekavirap
pāṭi vāmaṉacaṅkarappāṭiyumāka mūṉṟucceric caṅkarappāṭiyārum koṇṭa poṉ
Irupatiṉ kaḻañciṉāl muṉpu niṉṟa ceriyāre kaṭava non=tā viḷakkon
ṟum coḻarniyamattāraṭṭum Eṇṇai san=tiviḷakkerippatākavum Ira
ṇṭu śrīkoyiluḷ tevarkaḷai Uttaramayanasaṅgrān=tiyum cittirai viṣuvum sna
paṉamāṭṭuvataṟkum tiruviḻāviṟku viḷakkup piṭippāṟkum koṭi Eṭukkumāḷuk
kum tirumuṟṟam pukunta paruṣaināyaṉmāṟku Arici tuṇiyumRead tūṇiyumkoṣṭi ceytāṉu
kku Arici tuṇippaRead tūṇippatakkumtakkum pūjaṉaippoṉ Araikkaḻañcum maṟṟum śrīkoyiluḷ
kuṟaivuḷḷa nivantam pārāte kaṇṭaḻivile ceyvatākavum Ittevar śrī kā
Plate V b.

rdhyam Iṭaiyūṟuḷḷaṉa patiṉeṭṭu nāṭṭiyārume kaṭaikkaṇṭu tirantuRead tīrttukuṭuppā
rākavum Iññakarattu nakaramāḷvāṉum Āṭṭevāriyarum Eṟṟuvaḻicceriyārum
kañcakappāṭiyārum Ittevar vicam Aḻintatu Āṇṭutoṟum tiruviḻāc ceyta
vaḷave kaṇakku kāṇpatākavum Iccuṭṭappaṭṭa Iraṇṭu ceriyārume tevarpaṇ
ṭārattu vaitta nivantaṅ koṇṭu tirumeykāppu Iṭuvatākavum Ittevar śrī kāryya
m kaṭaikkāṇpāraiyum tirumeykāppāṉaiyum kaṇakkeḻutuvāṉai
yum nakarame javasthaiceytu Iṟaikoḷḷappeṟātatākavum śrīkoyi
lukku śrīkoyil nampāy nirampiṉāraippiṟātuviṭilRead ppeṟātuviṭilvetam valla brā
hmaṇaṉaiye Ārātikka Iṭuvatākavum Ipparicu Atikārañceyvāreva Aṟai O
lai ceyteṉ Iññakarattir virappāṭi madhyastaṉ nāṟpat teṇṇāyira maṅkalātitta
neṉ Eḻuttu Ikkaccippeṭṭu nakarattārpakkal vilaikoṇṭa nilam Oloka
mārāyap peṟuñceṟuvil kiḻakkil meṟ mikutikkuṟaivu Uṭpaṭa viṟṟukkuṭuttom mā
ṉakarattom Inta śāsanam Eḻuttu veṭṭiṉa Aṟantāṅki pormika viraṉāRead pormuka • only a few lines are wanting to complete the record; consequently a single plate appears to have been lost at the end.
Digital edition of SII 32, part 2, no. 204 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
